author: CC (design settled with Akien)
relates_to: the three /intent nodes born 2026-07-22 ‚Äî repo scanner, intentions-folder updater, bin dispatcher
text:
THE COMMAND IS `cairn` (Akien approved 2026-07-22). Measured, not assumed: `cc` is taken by /usr/bin/cc (the C compiler) AND shadowed by ~/.local/bin/cc, a DANGLING symlink to /home/akien/TheIgors/claudecode/cc.sh ‚Äî a launcher two generations dead, which is why `command -v cc` fell through to the compiler. Free on this box at time of measurement: cs, cr, ccs, cn, cai, ca, cx, cq, cz (scope: this machine, 2026-07-22 ‚Äî not a claim about other machines). Ruled out on judgment: `cs` (coursier, Scala), `ci`/`co` (real binaries from the rcs package), `crn` (one keystroke from `cron`). If a short alias is ever wanted, `cn` ‚Äî free here, no known collision, first and last letters of the word ‚Äî but as a personal shell alias, not a name the project asserts globally. `cairn` is self-describing for people who are not Akien, which Telos 3 (share these tools) requires, and `ca<TAB>` makes length a non-issue.

DISPATCH SHAPE. UU's form was `$UU_HOME/lab/claudecode/scripts/$1 $2 $3 ... $9`. Three defects not to carry over: it caps at nine arguments; it shreds quoting (any argument with a space becomes two); and it leaves an extra process in the tree. Correct form ‚Äî `verb=$1; shift; exec "$dir/$verb" "$@"` (Akien: CC++). 

DISCOVERY BY EXTENSION (Akien's design). Cairn shell commands are named `*.cairnshellcommand`, so `cairn help` runs `.../help.cairnshellcommand`, and the set needing help compiled is exactly `ls .../*.cairnshellcommand`. For each file found, pull the help text from its intention file. Compilation is mechanical ‚Äî the help is COMPILED, never written. This is the naming principle again (MAP: 'the name forces the content'): the extension makes the discovery query trivial and makes an undiscoverable command impossible to write by accident.

BIN IS A GATE, NOT A FOLDER. CLAUDE.md holds that a component without an intention doesn't run, so the dispatcher REFUSES to run a script with no charter beside it. That turns bin/ from a convenience into the enforcement point ‚Äî physics rather than a rule (Law 4). Bare `cairn` with no verb lists the verbs with their intentions, which costs nothing because it is the same compiled help.

OPEN (small): a `*.cairnshellcommand` and its intention must pair by an unambiguous rule ‚Äî filename convention (`help.cairnshellcommand` + `help.intention+why.json`) or a directory per verb. This is Law 5 at file granularity; the directory form matches how components already work, the flat form keeps bin/ scannable with one `ls`. Not decided.

SYMLINK TARGET: the updater links cairn/bin into ~/.local/bin (NOT ~/bin) and yanks stale links as it goes. MEASURED DEBRIS 2026-07-22 ‚Äî dangling: ~/.local/bin/cc, ~/bin/igor-worker, ~/bin/rescue-igor, ~/bin/typingmind, ~/bin/uucompactclaude. Live but pointing at the quarry: ~/bin/igor -> UnseenUniversity/igor. Also in ~/bin: uuhelp, uumytickets, _igor_common.sh/.ps1, igor.ps1, superclaude.ps1, compact-igor-type. Live and correct: ~/.local/bin/superclaude -> cairn/launchers/superclaude. Third-party, leave alone: claude, uv, uvx. AND: ~/.local/bin appears TWICE in PATH.

OPEN (needs Akien): is bin/ class-space (cairn/bin/) or is it what `launchers/` grows into? `launchers/` already exists doing symlink-onto-PATH work, and two folders doing one job is the drift this system exists to prevent.
